specific_mechanism: Tridosha theory: three fundamental bio-energetic constituents — vata (air-ether, governs movement and cognition), pitta (fire-water, governs transformation/digestion/heat), kapha (earth-water, governs structure/cohesion/lubrication). Each individual has a baseline prakriti (constitutional balance) and a current vikriti (present imbalance). Disease arises from drift between vikriti and prakriti. Treatment applies opposite-quality interventions (cooling for excess pitta, grounding for excess vata, energizing for excess kapha) rather than blanket suppression.
llm_application: An LLM agent has three operational doshas mapped as: vata-like exploration/movement tendency, pitta-like transformation/optimization tendency, kapha-like consolidation/preservation tendency. Each deployment has a baseline three-axis prakriti appropriate to its tenant context. Runtime measures current vikriti (per-axis drift); pathology = drift from prakriti, not absence of any single tendency. Intervention vocabulary is opposite-quality per-axis (cooling input to excess pitta-style agent, grounding context to excess vata-style agent), distinct from blanket safety filters or capability gates.
novelty_claim: Exploration-exploitation balance in LLM agents is heavily studied (Meta-RL 2512.16848, Exploration/Exploitation Errors 2604.13151, EPO 2509.22576, WESE 2404.07456) — but as a two-axis problem with scalar/policy-entropy interventions. The tridosha three-axis decomposition with per-axis baseline (prakriti) and opposite-quality counter-intervention vocabulary has not, to my knowledge, been ported to LLM agent constitution design. AI+Ayurveda exists for healthcare (AyuRAG, Swasthya AI, Arogya AI) but applies LLMs TO Ayurveda, not Ayurveda TO LLM design.
